sourceMovie.title: Mr. Holmes
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Mr. Holmes for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Bill Condon keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Sherlock: The Abominable Bride
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mr. Holmes clicked for you, Sherlock: The Abominable Bride (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Prestige
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mr. Holmes clicked for you, The Prestige (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mr. Holmes?
faq.answer: Try Sherlock: The Abominable Bride, The Prestige, Sherlock Holmes—each shares DNA with Mr. Holmes while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mr. Holmes?
faq.answer: Mr. Holmes (2015) is best described as Drama, Crime, Family, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mr. Holmes worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
